title: Interval-censored AFT model for laps-until-pit
summary: The prior research already pointed toward time-to-event reformulation, but the current best code is still plain binary next-lap classification.
rationale: The prior research already pointed toward time-to-event reformulation, but the current best code is still plain binary next-lap classification. XGBoost’s AFT objective supports interval-censored labels directly, which matches rows where the exact future stop lap is known in train and right-censoring can be assigned to terminal/no-further-stop rows. Survival-style targets often outperform myopic classifiers when the event hazard rises smoothly near the stop.
implementation_hint: For each train row, derive lower=upper=(next_pit_lap-current_lap) when a future stop exists, else lower=(remaining_laps+1), upper=+inf. Train `survival:aft` with race-group CV ordered by race chronology; convert predicted survival time into a next-lap probability via `P(T<=1)` or blend the rank of negative expected time with the current binary models.
expected_effect: Could add a genuinely different signal family and improve ranking near ambiguous medium-horizon stops where binary models flatten probabilities.
risk: If many rows have noisy or weakly informative event times, the AFT head may optimize survival likelihood without improving next-lap ROC AUC; conversion from time prediction to next-lap probability must be validated carefully.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
